prompt: Create a Definition of Done for a new Scrum team starting their first sprint. The team has 6 developers, 1 QA, and is building a B2B SaaS invoicing platform. They have basic CI but no CD pipeline yet.
assertions: produces a story-level DoD with 5-8 criteria appropriate for a new team
criteria are objectively verifiable, not vague (e.g., 'unit tests passing' not 'code is good')
includes code review, testing, and acceptance criteria verification at minimum
does NOT include deployment or monitoring criteria given no CD pipeline
recommends a maturity roadmap for adding criteria in future sprints
every criterion uses 'All work must...' or checklist phrasing
tags criteria sources with evidence tags (not solely [SUPUESTO])
output file named with {WIP} tag per naming convention

prompt: Design a multi-level Definition of Done for a regulated healthcare software project building an EHR integration. Must comply with HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and SOC 2 Type II. Team of 12 across 3 squads.
assertions: produces three distinct DoD levels: story, sprint, and release
story-level includes HIPAA-relevant criteria (PHI handling, encryption, access controls)
release-level includes FDA audit trail and electronic signature requirements
includes SOC 2 evidence collection criteria (logging, change management)
sprint-level includes cross-squad integration verification
provides a cascade/inheritance diagram (Mermaid or equivalent)
regulatory criteria cite specific regulation sections with [DOC] tags
distinguishes between automated checks and manual compliance reviews

prompt: Our sprint retrospective revealed that 40% of stories shipped in Sprint 8 did not meet our testing criteria — specifically, unit test coverage was below 80% and no integration tests were written. The team says time pressure is the cause. Audit this and recommend corrective actions.
assertions: performs a structured compliance audit, not just opinion
calculates per-criterion compliance percentages
classifies violations (skip vs. partial vs. waived vs. impossible)
investigates root causes beyond 'time pressure' — checks tooling, criterion clarity, estimation
recommends specific corrective actions with owners and deadlines
does NOT recommend simply removing the testing criteria
suggests systemic fixes (e.g., test scaffolding investment, definition clarity)
includes trend comparison recommendation against previous sprints

prompt: Our team has been using the same 12-item DoD for 18 months. We recently adopted a full CI/CD pipeline with automated deployments, implemented SAST scanning, and our peer review compliance has been 100% for 10 straight sprints. How should our DoD evolve?
assertions: recommends graduating peer review criterion since it is automated via branch protection
proposes adding deployment verification criteria now that CD exists
proposes adding SAST/security scan criteria leveraging new tooling
recommends adding observability/monitoring criteria for release-level DoD
warns against adding too many criteria at once — phases additions
provides a versioned evolution plan with target sprints
recommends a team consensus ceremony before changes take effect
maintains balance between raising the bar and avoiding bureaucratic bloat
